summary: No-ship for S4a: credential custody stores a secret-derived hint, and the userkey classify path bypasses the required gateway-client billing/allowance treatment.

findings.title: Secret-derived hint is stored and returned despite the sha256-only-at-rest invariant
findings.recommendation: Remove `secretHint` from the stored/listed key document, or replace it with a non-secret-derived display hint such as part of the sha256 key id. The plaintext key material should exist only in the mint response.

findings.title: Userkey `/classify` calls are not billed as `gateway-client` and have no userkey allowance gate
findings.recommendation: For `principal.kind === 'userkey'`, run the same owner allowance gate before the classifier call and route any billable LLM work through attribution that preserves `agentType: 'gateway-client'` and the owner/key context. Keep existing JWT/static behavior unchanged.
